About the Practice

Meet Dr. Mohana and Dr. Pedarsani

A husband and wife team who built SpecialEyes Optometry on a simple premise: every patient deserves thorough, honest eye care — without a long wait and without a large bill.

Tarek Mohana, O.D.

Optometrist & Co-Founder

Dr. Mohana was born and raised in Detroit, Michigan. He earned his undergraduate degree from the University of Michigan-Ann Arbor in 1997, then received his Doctorate of Optometry from the Illinois College of Optometry in Chicago in 2002.

After graduating, Dr. Mohana gained experience across a range of clinical settings before opening SpecialEyes Optometry in Lancaster in 2008 and expanding to Santa Clarita in 2009 — alongside his wife, Dr. Pedarsani.

His clinical focus includes contact lens fittings for complex prescriptions, LASIK candidacy evaluation and post-operative co-management, and the diagnosis and management of eye disease. Patients consistently describe him as warm, unhurried, and genuinely invested in their outcomes — qualities that have built a loyal patient base across the Santa Clarita Valley over more than 15 years.

Doctorate of Optometry — Illinois College of Optometry, 2002 B.S. — University of Michigan-Ann Arbor, 1997 Specialties: Contact Lenses, LASIK Co-Management, Eye Disease Languages: English, Arabic

Nasim Pedarsani, O.D.

Optometrist & Co-Founder

Dr. Pedarsani was born and raised in Southern California. She graduated from the University of California, Irvine in 1998, then completed her Doctorate of Optometry at the Illinois College of Optometry in Chicago in 2003.

Following graduation, Dr. Pedarsani completed a one-year residency at the West Side VA Hospital in Chicago, specializing in Ocular Disease and Low Vision — one of the most clinically intensive post-doctoral programs available to optometrists. After six years of practice in Chicago, she and Dr. Mohana returned to California to serve the community she grew up in.

Dr. Pedarsani is particularly known for her work with children and first-time patients. Parents regularly comment on her ability to make anxious kids feel comfortable, and her thorough, patient-centered approach has made her one of the most trusted optometrists in the Santa Clarita Valley. She is also fluent in Farsi and Spanish.

Doctorate of Optometry — Illinois College of Optometry, 2003 Residency in Ocular Disease & Low Vision — West Side VA Hospital, Chicago, 2004 B.S. — University of California, Irvine, 1998 Specialties: Pediatric Eye Care, Ocular Disease, Low Vision Languages: English, Spanish, Farsi

Our Practice

Why We Built SpecialEyes

SpecialEyes Optometry was founded in 2008 with one straightforward goal: bring thorough, efficient, and affordable eye care to communities that needed it. Dr. Mohana and Dr. Pedarsani chose to locate inside Costco Vision Centers deliberately — it keeps overhead low, passes savings to patients, and places eye care where families already shop.

Both doctors have clinical backgrounds that most community optometrists do not: VA Hospital training, pediatric clinic experience, and a residency in ocular disease and low vision. They bring specialist-level depth to a general practice setting.

Today SpecialEyes serves two locations — Santa Clarita and Lancaster — open six to seven days a week, with walk-ins always welcome. The same doctors who founded the practice are still seeing patients every day.

Meet the Doctors in Person

Schedule online or walk in — open 7 days in Santa Clarita, 6 days in Lancaster.
